adinnādānā.meaning: from taking what is not given; from theft
adinnādānā.gram: neuter compound noun, ablative singular (as in adinnādānā veramaṇī, "abstaining from taking what is not given")
adinnādānā.goenka: Adinnādāna, taking what is not given, is one of the ten unwholesome actions, and abstaining from it (adinnādānā veramaṇī) is the second precept of sīla. Observing this abstention is part of the moral foundation on which the meditator's samādhi and paññā are developed.

adiṭṭhā.gram: past participle of na passati (√dis), plural or feminine form

adubbhapāṇī.meaning: with a hand that does not hurt or deceive; harmless-handed

agamanīyaṭṭhānavītikkamacetanā.meaning: the volition of transgressing into a place one ought not to go; the intention to transgress with a forbidden partner
agamanīyaṭṭhānavītikkamacetanā.summary: A long compound analysing the factor of sexual misconduct: the cetanā (volition) of vītikkama (transgression) regarding an agamanīya-ṭhāna (a "place not to be gone to", i.e. a forbidden object).
breakdown: That ought to be gone to (future passive participle of gacchati).
Place, ground. (The doubled ṭṭh is sandhi doubling.)

agga.meaning: top, highest point; (in dhajagga) the top of a standard or banner
breakdown: Top, tip, highest point; foremost, supreme.
agga.summary: In the lexicon this entry explains the compound dhajagga: dhaja "banner, flag; mark, emblem" + agga "top, highest point", i.e. "top of a standard".

aggahesuṁ.gram: verb, 3rd person plural aorist of gaṇhāti (√gah)

aggamakkhāyati.meaning: is proclaimed the highest, is declared supreme
aggamakkhāyati.gram: compound verb: aggaṁ (accusative) + akkhāyati, passive, 3rd person singular present
breakdown: Is proclaimed, is declared (passive of akkhāti "to declare"). (The ṁ becomes m before a vowel.)

agganikkhitto.meaning: highly praised, famed; literally "laid down as the highest"
breakdown: Laid down, placed (past participle of nikkhipati).

aggaññasuttaṁ.meaning: the Aggañña Sutta, "the Discourse on What is Primary (on Origins)" (sutta title)
breakdown: Known as the highest or first; primeval, original (agga + -ñña, from √ñā).

agārasmā anagāriyaṁ.meaning: out of the house into homelessness (the formula of going forth)
breakdown: From the house, from home life (ablative singular of agāra).
Into homelessness (accusative of anagāriya: an + agāra + -iya).

ahañceva.meaning: and if I; I indeed thus
ahañceva.gram: sandhi fusion: pronoun + particles (ahaṁ + ca/ce + eva)
breakdown: Emphatic particle, "just, indeed". (The fusion gives -ñc- by sandhi.)

ahaññeveko.meaning: I alone, just I as one
ahaññeveko.gram: sandhi fusion: pronoun + particle + numeral adjective
breakdown: Emphatic particle, "just, only". (The fusion gives -ññ- by sandhi.)
ahaññeveko.note: uncertain: the lexicon's breakdown includes na ("not"), reading ahaṁ + na + eva + eko; DPD analyses the sandhi as ahaṁ + eva + eko. The sense in context is "just I alone".

ahetuka.meaning: without cause, groundless; having no reason

ahitakāmā.meaning: wishing harm; desiring what is not beneficial

ahitāya.meaning: not of any benefit; for (one's) harm

ahorattānusikkhī.meaning: training day and night; one who follows the training by day and by night

ahosī.gram: verb, 3rd person singular aorist of bhavati/hoti

ahu.gram: verb, 3rd person singular aorist of hoti (√hū)

ahuvattha.gram: aorist of hoti; the lexicon notes it as 3rd person singular middle, DPD as 2nd person plural
ahuvattha.note: uncertain: the lexicon parses this as 3rd person singular preterite middle of hoti; DPD parses it as aorist 2nd person plural. Both yield the past tense of "to be".

ahāsi.gram: verb, 3rd person singular aorist of harati (√har)

ajeḷakapaṭiggahaṇā.gram: neuter compound action noun, ablative singular

ajeṭṭhamaraṇaṁ-vāyaṁ.meaning: this one (dies) a death that is not the best; like one dying an ignoble death
ajeṭṭhamaraṇaṁ-vāyaṁ.gram: sandhi compound: a + jeṭṭha + maraṇaṁ + va + ayaṁ
breakdown: This one (demonstrative pronoun); fused by sandhi to -vāyaṁ.

ajeṭṭhamaraṇaṁvāyaṁ.meaning: this one (dies) a death that is not the best; like one dying an ignoble death
ajeṭṭhamaraṇaṁvāyaṁ.gram: sandhi compound: a + jeṭṭha + maraṇaṁ + va + ayaṁ (variant writing of the same phrase without hyphen)
breakdown: This one (demonstrative pronoun); fused by sandhi to -vāyaṁ.

ajini.gram: verb, 3rd person singular aorist of jināti (√ji)

ajjatagge.meaning: from this day forward, from today onwards
breakdown: Beginning, starting point (here: "with today as the start").

ajjhabhāsi.gram: verb, 3rd person singular aorist of adhibhāsati
breakdown: Prefix "to, towards"; before a vowel it becomes ajjh-.

ajjhattikabāhiresu.meaning: in the internal and the external
breakdown: Internal, personal, belonging to oneself (from ajjhatta).
Prefix; before a vowel it becomes ajjh-.
Prefix; before a vowel it becomes ajjh-.
Root "to carry"; with the prefixes, "to take down, swallow".

ajjhoharaṇādhippāyena.meaning: with the intention of eating or swallowing

ajjhājīve.meaning: as regards the way of life, concerning livelihood
breakdown: Prefix "with regard to"; before a vowel it becomes ajjh-.
Prefix "over, in"; before a vowel it becomes ajjh-.

ajjhāvasati dwell.note: uncertain: this key carries the English word "dwell" over from the source glossary's layout; the Pāli entry is ajjhāvasati "to dwell in, inhabit".
breakdown: Prefix "over, in"; before a vowel it becomes ajjh-.

ajjhāvasāma.gram: verb, 1st person plural present of ajjhāvasati
breakdown: Prefix "over, in"; before a vowel it becomes ajjh-.
ajjhāvasāma.note: divergence: the lexicon labels this form 2nd person plural; the ending -āma is the regular 1st person plural ("we dwell").

ajāyisuṁ.meaning: they were produced, they were born
ajāyisuṁ.gram: verb, 3rd person plural aorist of jāyati (√jan)
breakdown: Root "to be born, arise" (passive stem jāya-).

akammakāmā.meaning: not desiring work; averse to action

akantā.gram: past participle of na kāmeti (√kam), plural or feminine form
breakdown: Pleasant, desired, lovely (past participle of kāmeti).

akantāya.gram: past participle of na kāmeti, feminine oblique singular

akathito.meaning: not spoken of, not related, untold
akathito.gram: past participle of na katheti, masculine nominative singular

akatvākusalaṁ.meaning: not having done what is unwholesome
akatvākusalaṁ.gram: sandhi phrase: gerund + noun (a + katvā + akusalaṁ)
breakdown: The unwholesome. (katvā + akusalaṁ fuse to -vāku- by sandhi.)

akhaṇḍakāritāya.meaning: by unbroken performance; through practising without gap or breach
akhaṇḍakāritāya.gram: feminine abstract noun, instrumental singular, compound

akicchalābhī.meaning: attained without difficulty; one who gains (the meditative states) easily
breakdown: One who obtains, an attainer (from lābha "gain").

akiriya.meaning: non-action, non-doing; (as a doctrine) the denial of the efficacy of action

akittisañjananī.meaning: producing disrepute; generating loss of renown
